$MU launched a 256GB memory module built for AI data centers delivering faster LLM response times and far better power efficiency than traditional memory.

CPU memory is becoming the next bottleneck as AI moves from training to inference

$HOOD says customer balances in Robinhood Banking have reached $800M.

• Dec 2025 ~$100M
• Jan 2026 ~$300M
• Feb Earnings ~$400M
• Today ~$800M

Customers genuinely want Robinhood as their primary bank.

$EOSE CEO just purchased $345K worth of shares at $5.75.

A positive step toward rebuilding credibility but restoring trust will take time.

Michael Burry is reportedly long $ADBE.

Adobe is being priced as if AI disrupts it overnight but enterprise creative workflows don’t unwind that quickly which is the kind of contrarian setup Burry tends to favor.
$GOOGL breaking below $300
$SE DOWN ~25%  😳

Real estate giant $BX down 5% after redemption requests at its $82B private credit fund reached ~8% of assets in Q1 which is well above the 5% threshold where limits can apply.

Another signal of continued pressure across the broader $2T private credit market.

$NVDA GTC kicks off March 16 with Jensen Huang keynoting before ~30K attendees.

Jensen called GTC “the epicenter of the AI industrial era” framing AI as essential infrastructure advancing across every layer of the stack.

$TEM expanded its multi-year partnership with $MRK to deepen AI-driven biomarker and precision oncology research.

Merck will use Tempus’ multimodal datasets and GPU-backed Lens platform reinforcing that pharma is moving AI into core drug development workflows.
$AMZN data center division has agreed to acquire George Washington University’s Virginia Science and Technology Campus in Ashburn for ~$427M.

$NBIS just proved AI data centers don’t have to be “always on.”

In a UK trial with National Grid & $NVDA, a 96-GPU Blackwell cluster cut power use 40% in under a minute while workloads stayed live which suggests compute can become dispatchable instead of purely incremental.

$SOFI is rolling out SoFiUSD for settlement across $MA network with Galileo giving clients access to stablecoin rails via Mastercard’s Multi-Token Network.

The goal is 24/7 remittance and B2B settlement as global stablecoin volume now exceeds $30B per day.

Memory chipmakers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix each plunged more than 10%.

The sharp selloff marked what’s being called a “Black Tuesday” for South Korea’s stock market.

$ASTS is targeting ~$1B in annual revenue by 2027 with expanding government exposure and no plans for additional convertible issuance.

It also now has 50+ global MNO partners covering ~3B subscribers and ~1,150 MHz of secured spectrum worldwide.

The SPX is now down ~2.5%.

As the red annotations show, the 10-year yield is 5 bps lower to 4.06%.

Bonds are being bought as crash insurance.

This probably means stocks have to crash to keep bonds from trading back to 4.11%.

Yesterday, the national average gasoline price jumped 11.2 cents (bottom panel).

This exceeded the largest one-day jump after the start of the Russia-Ukraine War in March 2022 of 10.9 cents.
 
Only the early September 20025 one-day jump of 19 cents was larger. This was when Category 5 Hurricane Katrina tore up oil infrastructure in the Gulf around New Orleans.
---
Keep in mind, this is the beginning of the month. So starting the month with a big daily jump in gasoline prices could lead to a huge increase in Energy CPI for March. 

Will it be rationalized away? Yes. But if you want the Fed to cut rates, things just got a lot more complicated if energy CPI pushes headline CPI to a big number in March.

While futures are flashing panic red, the signs were there over the past few weeks:

1. We have had 4/5 VIX spikes over 19

2. Financials have rolled over hard, usually a market signal

3. Energy turning bullish weeks ago

As these unfortunate events in the Middle East have unfolded, I came into this week cautiously positioned:

1. About 9% cash

2. Overweight Industrials (more aero/defense), Staples and Energy

3. Massively underweight Financials, underweight Tech

I will be focusing on two things today - patience and process

For long-context inference, Nvidia’s $NVDA GB300 delivered between 1.33X to 1.87X higher throughput per GPU with ~1.5X lower latency versus the GB200 in both high-throughput and latency-throughput balanced scenarios, per LMSYS.

$AMD $AVGO

Nvidia $NVDA is said to be preparing a return to consumer PCs with AI SoCs, combining CPUs from MediaTek and Intel with its GPUs. 

Dell and Lenovo are said to be preparing models with the Nvidia-MediaTek chip with availability as soon as the first half this year. 

$DELL $AMD $INTC $ARM

📺 MARKETS ON THE EDGE: WRITE DOWN THESE LEVELS

While many expected a sharp selloff on Monday, it didn’t materialize right away. Instead, there was a temporary sense of stability.

However, volatility has returned, and the market is testing a critical range that has been in place since October.

$SPY is sitting around $674–$675 — a major technical pivot. This level marks the upper boundary of a multi-month range. The market has been compressing here for months, and a break below could trigger a meaningful move.

Key levels:
$SPY
– $675 → Current range pivot, must hold to maintain range
– $662 → Roughly 5% off the highs, first real downside target
– $650 → About 6.8% off highs

$QQQ
– $593 → Major support
– $585 → 200-day moving average
– $580 → Critical weekly support; break and hold likely signals further downside below

Focus on these levels. Write them down. When volatility spikes, your emotions won’t help you — preparation will.

It is also early for the “Blood in the Street” account, which deploys capital in tiers during deeper corrections (5–6% off highs and in structured tiers).

At current levels (~$675), it’s too early. If $662 or below comes into play, then it becomes actionable.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
$qqq weekly chart for some perspective”If” ranges break
